meaning: adj.象猫头鹰的,面孔严肃的,
examples: With his owlish face, it is easy to understand why he was called "The Professor."
"Everyone gets a piece of the action, " says DiBello, who has deep-set owlish eyes and a Philadelphia-accented baritone.
He looks grey, owlish, bespectacled and glum. If the Republicans are looking for a jolt of inspiration and a fresh face to lead them into battle, Mr McConnell is not their man.
Nuclear spokespeople in every other country will then spout owlish and well-financed explanations of why it cannot happen to them.
Other money managers, longs and shorts alike, discuss stocks with owlish aplomb.
